name: Structured Exercise Training
summary: Supervised or prescribed aerobic, interval, resistance, or combined exercise programs tracked as the main human intervention class for mitochondrial biogenesis and dynamics in older adults.
mechanism_summary: Exercise training can activate skeletal-muscle mitochondrial adaptation through PGC-1alpha-linked biogenesis, mitochondrial protein synthesis, oxidative-enzyme abundance, respiration, and fusion/fission quality-control pathways, while also improving cardiorespiratory fitness, muscle mass, insulin sensitivity, or strength depending on modality and dose.

open_questions: Which modality and intensity best preserve mitochondrial function in frail, multimorbid, or mobility-limited older adults
How durable mitochondrial and functional gains remain after detraining or lower adherence
Which pharmacologic co-interventions blunt or amplify exercise-driven mitochondrial adaptation
Whether mitochondrial improvements predict morbidity, disability, or mortality beyond established exercise benefits
